items.title: I've Made Too Much Pasta
items.description: I've Made Too Much Pasta is a furry indie rock band with a bunch of original music about raccoons and trash and stuff.  It is not a cooking panel.  We sing about important issues like rabies, stealing cable, raccoons, and your parents leaving you at Disneyworld.  There will be a lot of noises and singing, and some shoegaze parts.  If you come to this you can dance and jump around.  I guess you can do that even if you don't come.  Also, do not yell "Freebird" in the audience, it's not funny anymore.

Come and watch a presentation on the dos and don’ts of personal finance, including tips on investing, budgeting, and smart shopping. After the presentation, we’ll play Kahoot with prizes at stake!

items.description: Curious about BDSM but are unsure how to safely participate? Don't know what those fancy terms mean? Just have some questions? Come by for quick lesson in the basics and chat!

Join the panelists in exploring how writing groups helped them grow, their experiences, and what you might expect from a writing group you join or start.

items.title: From UwU to OwO: How Languages Change
items.description:
Have you ever wondered why people talk like that? 

In this panel, we’ll chat about how languages change over time. Why does slang develop? What makes an accent? When does one language become two? What the heck is “6 7”?

If you’ve ever wanted to learn about historical linguistics, this is a great introduction to the field!

items.title: canceled - How to be You, Everywhere
items.description:
"Are you lost, trying to figure out who you are?
Do you feel like you can't be yourself? 
Do you feel like you have to be someone you're not?

Timmy used to feel the same way. Every day we tell ourselves little lies about who we have to be, how we have to feel, what we have to do. In this panel, Timmy will teach you how to be yourself, and that there is no such thing as have to.

Only have to if you want to ___.

You have to attend if you want to hear Timmy talk about this. <3"

items.title: Confessions of a Greymuzzle
items.description:
“Furry has no official birthday,” says Joe Strike, author of Furry Nation: The True Story of America’s Most Misunderstood Subculture, and Furry Planet: A World Gone Wild. “But it was most likely born at mid-1980s sci-fi conventions when fans of anthropomorphic animals gathered after hours to share furry art and talk about their favorite cartoons.
  
“I wasn’t present at furry’s creation, but I discovered it on “day three” as it were.
Actually, furry discovered me in 1988 when I received a surprise invitation to the very first east coast furry party at a Philadelphia sci-fi convention.

“In the decades since I’ve watched furry grow from a few hundred in the US to a global phenomenon; furry conventions now take place in numerous countries, attracting thousands of people across the planet—and most of them, like myself were already fascinated with anthro animals before discovering they were part of a worldwide community!”

"Confessions of a Greymuzzle" (a term used to describe older furs) takes a wide-ranging look at furry’s history, including:
-	How it all began: the forgotten “funny animal” comic books that inspired the first furry artists;
-	Invasion of the fursuiters
-	The media take notice (for better—but mostly for worse)
-	Respectability: just around the corner—maybe?

Just two best friends trying to build their dream in the culinary industry, and need the input of the community.

We want to set up a free photoshoot for anyone and everyone interested in a yearbook-style photo.

In return, we ask that anyone who is interested in participating in a survey to help us find our demographic and see what the community wants.

Everyone is invited and welcomed, from friends to family, solo to groups, etc. 
Props are allowed in the photos (as long as they follow the rules and guides of the convention)

Can't wait to see you there!

NOTE: This event is not directly affiliated with Anthrocon, but rather a submitted event like most panels.

items.title: PRKC Hosts: Petplay Mosh
items.description:
Now’s your chance to gear up and paw-ty like a rockstar with other Anthrocon PetPlayers! A Mosh is a PetPlay event set up like a classic dog park, with toys to play with and mats to roll around on! The difference in a Primrose Kennel Club (PRKC) hosted Mosh and many regular Moshes, is in the *Foundation of Consideration!*

For starters, we “Consider All Types of Pets at PRKC”- including toys for all manner of animals, a divided “soft” and “rough” Play space, and a wristband “play consent” system for Pets to wear during play. PRKC Moshes also provide roll-room, knee-cushions, and easier-to-grab toys for Wheelchair-Bound Pets. Prim instructs hearing-impaired-friendly “Paw Signals” with Pets and Handlers before and during the Mosh to ensure communication happens in and out of Headspace. 

At a PRKC Mosh, we constantly monitor our players to ensure they are respecting others’ consent and boundaries, as well as their health and safety. We’re not afraid to put you in the Dog House (and/or report you to Security) for being disrespectful, inappropriate, or making others uncomfortable. Remember: Kink and Fetish Events ≠ Sexual Consent, so please do not attend with the intent of “meeting a hookup.”

Don’t be shy, come out and play! We can’t wait to meet you!

items.title: Chasing Weather Balloons
items.description:
Panel Meets near the Elevator, across the DLCC 3rd floor Skybridge near Art Show and will move to the roof from there. 


Like a scavenger hunt? How about from the edge of space?

Twice a day, weather balloons rise high into the atmosphere before bursting and drifting back to Earth. Most people never notice—but the data they collect is critical to the forecasts we rely on every day. Some of us go find them.

This panel is your introduction to the world of weather balloon chasing: what these balloons are, why they’re launched, and how they quietly power modern weather prediction.

We’ll show you how to track them in real time using radio signals and GPS data, predict where they’ll land, and what it takes to actually recover one on the ground. Along the way, we’ll break down the radiosondes they carry and the data they transmit from high above.

To bring it all together, we’ll track a live launch from the Pittsburgh National Weather Service and walk through the process step by step.

Part science, part radio, part scavenger hunt—come see what’s falling out of the sky, and how to go get it.

items.title: Plumage Peepin' - An Intro to Birdwatching!

Birds are all around us, all the time, in pretty much every place on earth (except Antarctica of course). From city dwelling critters like Rock Pigeons and House Sparrows, elusive owls, wacky woodcocks, curious corvids, and more—there's birds out there for everyone! All one has to do is stop for a moment and take a look at what's around them!

This panel will cover the basics of birdwatching such as:

 - Useful websites and apps for your phone.
 - Cheap gear if you want to go further!

items.title: Tying The Knot: Basic Rope Rigging for Furries
items.description: Believe it or not, this is a 100% Safe-For-Work panel... In fact, safety while working is the bottom line when it comes to this topic! This interactive panel is for all those who want to learn the absolute basics of ropes, rigging, and the various types of rope and equipment used in the fire/rescue services. We'll be examining rope access equipment and basics from an absolute beginner perspective, including: Discussing basic Personal Protective Equipment needed for rope rigging; Discussing the various types of rope/webbing, their uses, and safety considerations; Practicing how to properly tie some of the basic knots (figure 8, overhand, water knot) used in the fire service; Demonstrating how ropes can be used to construct basic mechanical advantage haul systems to move a load from one point to another; and Having an opportunity to examine, handle, and ask questions about equipment used by rope technicians, tower climbers, firefighters, and other rope access personnel. This panel is not shibari-related, but we hope it will give you a newfound understanding of the use of rope to accomplish movement and hauling in a safe way.

I scream, you scream, we all scream* because we’re goats!

items.title: How To Not Get Scammed Online
items.description:
A friend got scammed out of $600 - I don't want that to happen to you.

I'm a cybersecurity administrator for the Department of Defense, and I invite you to my cybersecurity panel! I will teach you how to fend off the various online scams specifically targeting us furries and our friends. I will show you real-world examples of what the scams look like, how the scammer operates, how to defeat them, and how you can protect yourself and your friends-so that we can all make sure no one else gets robbed of their convention or their money ever again!

No experience, degree, technical knowledge, or certifications required! Come as you are and get ready to learn!

items.title: Rodent Meet & Greet
items.description: Do you skitter and scurry around? Do you have the cutest incisors? Then the rodent meet-and-greet is for you! Come meet your fellow rodents, talk about your characters, and hopefully meet new friends. Group photo at the end and rodent lovers and sister species are welcome.

items.title: Wheel of Furtune & Furry Jeopardy!
items.description:
Furry game show fans: Kijani’s Game Show Extravaganza is back at Anthrocon! We will play two classic shows with a furry twist, Wheel of Furtune & Furry Jeopardy! Contestants are chosen out of the audience via qualifying rounds & contestant exams prior to each show. Great prizes will be won, including free reg for AC 2027 and a custom Cryptid-themed mini blanket! Each show will last about one hour (including qualifying rounds). Don’t miss out on this authentic game show experience!

NOTE: If you want to try out for Wheel of Furtune, make sure to arrive early as there are only 27 qualifying spots available. Tickets for Wheel qualifiers will start to be handed out prior to entering the room.

items.title: Transit and Furbanism
items.description: Interested in cars, trains, buses, bikes, urban planning, and how cities function? Come meet other furry urbanists and learn about urbanism, Pittsburgh transit, and how transit and good urbanism creates a better fandom! Join us after the panel for a ride to East Liberty on the East Busway.

items.title: Free Miniature/DND Paint & Take!
items.description:
Provided by Chronicle RPGs & Zenith's Outer Reach; Come paint with us! Whether you're a complete beginner or an established maestro, join us for a free miniature and all supplies provided. Take part for as long as you like with drop-in tables commencing from 10AM! 

Scan the QR code to preview the Zenith's Outer Reach Kickstarter, or copy the link here: https://www.backerkit.com/call_to_action/f895d526-7872-41e5-94c1-a010a6ec5898/landing?preview_mode=true

items.title: Floor Wars Preliminaries
items.description: It's time for the Annual Floor Wars Prelims! If your team has signed up online, you must compete in the preliminaries in order to qualify for the main event. There are NO sign ups in-person. For group performances and pre-approved exceptions for the Fursuit Dance Competition, they will be covered in this event.

items.title: Mario Kart 8 Tournament Check In
items.description: Registration is at https://www.start.gg/tournament/paws-of-fury-2026-at-anthrocon/

items.title: The Cycle of Ephemera
items.description: A convention booklet is meant to last a weekend. A wildflower, only a few days. A life? Only a blink in the eyes of the universe. In this panel, we explore Convention Ephemera-those fragile, physical fragments of our history that are often lost to time. In line with the mission of archivists, we discuss why documenting the "temporary" is our most vital task. From the badge in your hand to the legacy of our community, join us to learn how we "save" the moments that weren't meant to last forever, ensuring our shared history isn't erased by the cycle of ephemera.

items.title: Fuzzy Tales Presents: Camera, Action, Darkness
items.description: Welcome to Appalachia in the 1990s as you and your friends come to chase down a local legend to put on film for the World to see that exists in the wilderness nearby. Despite the rumors of previous people vanishing in their pursuit, that was not going to stop you from an Oscar winning film. Will you find the Creature and create your winner or vanish like the others before you.

items.title: DIY Leathercraft: Leather Dice Bags
items.description: Want to make a custom bag for all of your math rocks? In this workshop, we will teach you the basics of leatherworking, and leave you with a small dice bag crafted by your own hands. There will be a 30 dollar materials fee to participate, but any may observe for free.

items.title: For the Hive : Drones and Hives in the Furry space
items.description:
"LOGIN | SYNC-UP | CONNECT

Are you curious what it is like to surrender control to a collective, or what it is like to exist as an asset rather than individual? Then please come, learn from us and others as we discuss the dynamics of Hive existence. Drone-curious or fully integrated. All are welcome! 

The temporary hive is gathering point for the engineered, the augmented, and the constructed. Whether you run on nanite-streams, scaled bioframes, soft-fur chassis, fully digital architecture, or curious and looking in at the edges, come and meet other like-minded beings. And don't worry, the temp-hive's firewalls are hardened to repel even the most persistent rogue code. Join us, for the Hive!

items.title: Cackle Of Yeens
items.description: A Meet and Greet for everyone in the Hyaenidae Family! From Spotted Hyenas, Striped Hyenas, Brown Hyenas and Aardwolfs. Come by to meet, chat, and become friends with these wonderful creatures in the fandom! Suiters and Nonsuiters are welcome as well as other species!

items.title: Pokémon TCG Meetup
items.description: Whether you are a collector, a casual player or a competitive player: join us for a nice get-together of like-minded fluffs for our favorite trading card game! Here you can chat, trade and play with other trainers. Or, if you are not yet familiar with the game behind the cards, we can teach you how the game works! If you are looking to go pro with PTCG, we can certainly help you with that too. Even folks outside of the trading card game can join us to talk about all things pokémon!

items.description: Iggy's classic game of secret words!  Each round has 5 passwords you give 1 word clues to your partner.  All five passwords in a round are clues towards a riddle.  Guess the riddle, win the round!

items.title: Avali Meet and Greet
items.description: Calling all fluffy space raptors, Avalon needs you! Let's use our collective one avali brain cell to destroy everyone who calls us "chicken"! Bring your pack, or be prepared to find new pack mates at this meet and greet. Prepare for a collection of the galaxy's silliest, BIG avali and possibly cool stickers too.

items.title: How to Handle your Fursuiter
items.description: Hey there reader! Are you a friend, parent, or significant other that has been dragged to a fur con in Pittsburgh and are super confused on what to do and where to go? Well I have just the panel for you. Come on down and learn how to take care of your fuzzy friend while getting a lay of the land!

items.title: Macro/Micro Meet and Greet
items.description: Attention creatures of all shapes and sizes! Join us for a one-hour social event for the biggest and smallest folks in the fandom. Whether you're a giant wolf, a tiny tiger, a huge hoofer, or an enormous eel, you belong here!

items.title: Albedo: Platinum Catalyst
items.description: The tabletop game inspired by and dedicated to the epic, soft-core sci-fi militaristic comic universe created by Steve Gallacci, which was actually a major influence in the initial coming-together of the Furry Fandom! Does your unit have the wit, grit and spit in order to overcome the invading Independent Lapine Republic forces that have come with xenocentric means of universal domination?

items.title: Busts and Butts: Fursuit Padding!
items.description: Animal shapes, Gender shapes, Sewn-in padding, and Juicy haunches! This panel is all the tips and tricks you’ll need to get that perfect fursuit padding shape! Come ask questions, look at WIPs, and learn the step-by-step of fursuit padding construction.

items.title: Golden Treasure: The Lost Draak
items.description: A small TTRPG by SteelTheWarrior made in tribute to the inspiring and breathtaking game “Golden Treasure: The Great Green”.    Players will take the form of a group of Draak-Kin hatched amid the chaos of their nestlands being torn asunder by No-Tailed creatures that pay no respect to the Nature they take from. The team will find No-Tails, predators, starvation, mazes, treacherous weather and even competing Draak all standing between the hatchlings and hope for survival!  Will they survive, or feed the cycle?

items.title: Everything You Wanted to Know About Albedo
items.description: Discussing Albedo and other early furry comics and history

items.title: Fox in the Coffee Shop - Slice of Life
items.description: Sometimes a story doesn’t require a princess to be saved, or an adventure to be taken. Occasionally, stories look at trying to show a bit of the drama, comedy, or tragedy in everyday life. Our panelists will help you make even this seemingly mundane genre interesting and exciting, and look at ways to keep your story from being just another Wednesday.

items.title: Fursuiting 101 with Gale Frostbane
items.description: Do you want to bring your fursuit to life? Watch fursuiters do funny things? Watch the fursuiters and audience make the host's head spin with chaos? Its time for Fursuiting 101! This panel is focused on fun and laughing while learning! Lose yourself in a world of funny animals and let yourself play pretend!

items.title: DLCC - Rooftop Hours
items.description: The DLCC Rooftop closes to attendees at 11pm sharp. Attendees must leave the rooftop and overlooks by that time.

items.title: Stayin’ Alive in SPAAAAAACE: How to Survive
items.description:
You may remember the Basics of Rocket Science panel last year, and if you want to learn more about space, I’m your cat! Join me to learn how we send people up into the black and bring them back safe and sound!

This is an entry level science communication event suitable for enthusiasts and those curious about the topic. We’ll cover the history of life support in space as well as promising new technologies.

items.title: Creative Growth!: Meet Furry Size Artists!
items.description: Love “King-Size Canary?” Lived for That Scene in Charlotte’s Web? Macro, inflation, and fat furries have been a big part of furry since the beginning. Come ask size-themed creators anything!

items.title: SCP & PPAC Pet Romp TEARDOWN
items.description: Breakdown for the Mosh

items.title: Furry Choir OPEN Rehearsal
items.description:
Come one come all to Anthrocon Furry Choir rehearsal! You don't need to read music, you don't need to be a ""good singer"", and we want YOU.
There will be a performance of 3-4 songs later in the day, time and place TBA.

Sheet music available here: https://tinyurl.com/mvpa6wfs

items.title: 90 Years of Cons: Philcon 1936 to Anthrocon 2026
items.description: 90 years ago a group of fans from New York visited fans in Philadelphia, starting the first con which paved the way for Worldcon. In the last 90 years cons have evolved from those small regional meet ups to media juggernauts pulling in thousands of attendees. Philcon also played a role in the formation of Anthrocon! Join us for a 90 year journey from the small science fiction cons of First Fandom leading to the formation of comic, gaming, anime, furry, and other cons sprung from the party floor. It’s all one giant family tree of fandoms and conventions. Ursa Award winning panel.

items.title: Blood on the Clocktower
items.description: Blood on the Clocktower is a social deduction game. There are two teams; good (townsfolk and outsiders) and evil (minions and demon). You will be assigned a random role that has a power that you can use throughout the game to deduce who among you is the demon, to find and execute them, or if you are evil you must eradicate the town before the demon is executed. Alas, death is not the end in Blood on the Clocktower. You still play the game after you are killed, winning or losing with your team.

items.title: Four Stooges Play A Wine Bar (again!)
items.description:
A scientifically-inclined cockroach, an otherworldly ferret, a tipsy badger, and a spicy triangle dog walk into City Winery...Join this cadre of comedians for an evening of comedy and music away from the hustle and bustle of the convention center. Relax, have some food and a beverage at City Winery's breathtaking performance venue while our curious quartet brings the fun of Anthrocon to a brand new space!


DOORS OPEN AT 6PM

City Winery in Pittsburgh: 1627 Smallman St., Pittsburgh, PA 15222

Google map link: https://maps.app.goo.gl/fZyHNN2BZbr1b17t5

items.description: Real lawyers, a ferret judge, and a furrified case!  Join Boozy, Buddy, and the Honorable Alkali Bismuth for a mock trial performance where things may get strange, they may get funny, and they'll always be barely legal at best!

items.title: Fursuit Parade
items.description: Fursuit Parade

items.title: Ezo (Friday Nightly Dance)
items.description:
Every night of the convention, our main stage transforms into a world of lights, effects, and electronic music.

Friday (Saturday Morning) 12:30am to 1:30am
DJ: Ezo
Genre: hard techno
items.location: DLCC - Spirit of Pittsburgh Ballroom (Main Events)

items.title: So You Wanna Buy Some Art: Tips on Commissioning
items.description: Have an awesome idea for a con badge, illustration, short story, or fursuit? Don’t have the time or skill to pull it off yourself? We are fortunate to be part of a fandom saturated with talented creatives who can make your dreams a reality - but commissioning someone for the first time can be daunting! Come learn how to be an awesome client and get awesome art!

items.title: Anthropomorphic Characters in Silent Film
items.description: Anthropomorphic characters in film are not a modern invention. From animation to film, the silent movie era had more than its share of memorable characters. Oswald the Lucky Rabbit and Felix the Cat are two of the most famous examples but there is so much more. Now over one hundred years later, learn how these early cinematic examples of Anthro animals laid the groundwork for the fandom of today.

items.title: Pony Play 101 & Meetup
items.description:
Puppies aren't the only kinky animal out there!

Ever wanted to feel the tug on your bridle?  Or maybe have a pretty harnessed pony at your beck and call?

Join Pony Freyja, the 2026 North American Pony title holder, to learn some of the basics about pony play and meet some of your fellow horse-curious!  We'll be discussing all sorts of fun activities to get up to both as a pony and a handler, showing some gear, and teaching the basics of being on either end of the reins, including how to adapt for accessibility.  Come find your herd!

items.title: Beta Reading & Critique
items.description: At some point in our writing journy, we have to show our work to others, or look at someone else's work to offer feedback. So what do we say? How do we help make a story the best it can be? This panel will help guide you through working with others stories.

items.title: Beyond Tamagotchi: The World of Virtual Pets
items.description: Tamagotchis aren't the only virtual pets on the block! Come learn about bunny roms, GigaPets, and more virtual animals you can keep on a keychain. Enter the digital world with Tig!

items.title: Rock Hounds
items.description: This panel is for folks of all backgrounds who are in any way interested in geology. This could include everyone from curious individuals to folks who are currently working in geosciences. The panel will include a brief presentation on regional Western Pennsylvania Geology (made for general audiences, nothing hyper technical), along with some display samples from around the area. Afterwards there will be a little rock swap, and some free Black Topaz Crystals from the SK Star Claim No. 2 in Juab County, Utah for those who pop in. It's recommended (but not required in any way), to bring samples of your own to trade with others, though they certainly don't have to be anything special.

items.title: Bodybuilding 101: Becoming a Furry Bara Beef Boy
items.description:
Do you like Muscle? Want to learn how to get huge IRL and meet other furs with the same iron bug? Whether you’re a complete beginner or seasoned lifter, this panel has it all: Training, Diet, Supplementation, and Lifestyle. Taught by the Muscle Fursuit Maker himself, Dozer will leverage his 15+ years of training experience to deliver the most informative and fun panel of your weekend.  Meet and Greet will follow.

Note: All are welcome!
Note2: This is Bodybuilding Panel, not a Bara Art Panel

items.title: Rock Climbing Meet Up & Excursion
items.description:
-OFFSITE - MEET UP IN FRONT OF THE WESTIN HOTEL

"Interested in rock climbing? So are we!
Whether you’re a seasoned climber or touching the wall for the very first time, come join a friendly group of furries for a bouldering meet-and-greet at a local gym. We’ll gather as a group, enjoy a one-mile walk to FA Climbing Station Square, snap a group photo, and cheer each other on as we climb together!

Event details:
Day passes start at $19
Rental shoes are available on-site
A waiver must be completed before climbing
For safety, fursuit heads are not permitted while climbing

Come hang out, make new friends, and have a fun, active experience, no matter your skill level!

items.title: Convention Center Access Hours
items.description: The Hours that the David L. Lawrence Convention Center will be accessible by members of the public.

items.title: Engineering After Dark (18+)
items.description: Are you curious about the wild and freaky inventions that furries build for their most intimate encounters? Or maybe you have an idea for a contraption those curious furries need to hear? Come and join two experienced professionals from the after-dark engineering community as they discuss their favorite kinky inventions, how to talk to other people about your own designs and finally, how to bring those designs into the real world. Construction paper and colored pencils will be provided.

items.title: Fursuit Price is Right
items.description: This is the fursuiter version of The Price Is Right, where fursuiters are called to "Come On Down!" to Contestant's Row.  Winner plays a pricing game and goes on to The Showcase Showdown, where the winner of which goes on to the showcase.  The fursuiter that has won the most in cash and prizes wins a badge commission.

items.title: Artists' Lounge
items.description:
A chill drawing space!

Sponsored by Part-Time Dragons

items.title: Furry History
items.description:
A brief tour of the history of the furry fandom. Or: how we went from a room party to the Guinness Book of World Records.

Do you wonder where this all started and how? Here's an overview of how the fandom and cons such as Anthrocon came about.

items.title: Anthrocon Poker Championship - Final Table
items.description:
Paws of Fury returns for 2026 with 7 charity gaming events! Event #4 is the 11th annual Anthrocon Poker Championship! Can you dodge bullets to win this year's title and add your name to the Silver Water Dish?

Day 1 of the event will play down to our July 9, which takes place Friday at 3 PM.

Entry Fee: $20 charitable donation
30 dog tags will be available for purchase for $30, and provide one rebuy
Prizes for the top 9 places

Full Event Details and Registration: https://www.start.gg/tournament/paws-of-fury-2026-at-anthrocon/event/anthrocon-poker-championship
(Note: You must have your con badge number to register for the event and must be 18 years of age or older)

The final table will be streamed at http://www.twitch.tv/AnthroconGaming

items.title: Marching Band Rehearsal
items.description:
Closed Rehearsal for the Fursuit Marching Band
INFO: https://linktr.ee/FurryMusicProgram

items.title: Dancing with Disabilities
items.description: Many people are disabled in the furry fandom, and many want to be able to get on the stage and dance in the dance comp or even floor wars, but where do you start if you're disabled? Well, look no further! I am neph, and I am a disabled dancer! In this panel I will talk about how I learned to get on the dance floor and work with my disabilities to make dancing easier! I will be helping others who may wish to get on the stage as well, so they can learn to work with their disability on stage!

items.title: Your Imagined Furry Fantasy 18+
items.description: This light-hearted RPG system was borne of frisky fantasy, silly stories of over-the-top shenanigans, inspired by popular flash games like Corruption of Champions, Trials in Tainted Space and Flexible Survival! Gameplay gives players a different perspective on the psychology behind modern-day sexuality and taboo as they adventure in a world where just about everything is over-sexualized (including combat!) to offer silly-fun and sex-positive escapades!

items.title: Win, Lose or Draw Charity Edition
items.description: Calling all quick thinkers, fast sketchers, and loud guessers! Win, Lose, or Draw: Charity Edition brings two teams of three head-to-head in a whirlwind of silly drawings, frantic guessing, and audience surprises. Think classic game show energy—with con-style fun and furry chaos sprinkled in. Every right guess boosts the con's charity total, so come help us break our previous record! No talent? No problem. We celebrate glorious scribbles here.

items.title: General Art Show Auction
items.description: The General-Audiences Art Show Auction

items.title: Milo's Notebook
items.description: Join Little Alpha Pup as he takes to the stage once again to bring you an original musical featuring Broadway Classics, Golden-Era Disney, and Original Music! A heartwarming and tear-jerking story of love, heartbreak, and getting back up again, told through song and narration that will leave you feeling a sense of hope. Originally debuted at Megaplex '24 with great reception, and since performed at Midwest Furfest, Nordic Fuzzcon, Eurofurence, Texas Furry Fiesta, and more, this jukebox musical is a must-see for all who love our community!

items.title: Don’t Die: The Lazy Creature’s Guide to Health
items.description:
Taking care of yourself sounds great in theory… but what if you’re tired, busy, or just really committed to the couch? This panel is all about low-effort ways to stay reasonably healthy without turning your life into a strict routine. We’ll talk about tiny habits, simple food choices, easy movement, sleep tricks, and other realistic strategies that help your body function better without demanding huge amounts of motivation.

No intense workout plans, no complicated diets, and no guilt—just practical ideas designed for normal people, busy creatures, and professional loungers alike. If your long-term health strategy is mostly “please don’t die,” this panel will give you some surprisingly doable ways to improve your odds.

items.title: Enamel Pin Trading Party
items.description: Pin Pals Unite! Got enamel pins to trade, swap, or show off? Join the ultimate Enamel Pin Trading Party! Discover unique designs, make new connections, and find that perfect pin to complete your collection. Whether you’re a seasoned trader or just getting started, this is your chance to trade not only Enamel pins but furry items for pins too!

items.title: "Dragons, Cryptids, and Dinos, OH MY!"
items.description: Learn how to craft vibrant, exotic creature characters for your stories and artwork by studying zoology, paleontology, folklore, history, and a menagerie of other authors’ work. We will also dissect a dragon—from inspiration, to designing, to writing his story and crafting a maquette for drawing. A Q & A session will follow.

items.title: Jump to FTL! - Science Fiction
items.description: Take some Time And Relative Discussions In Space to talk all things sci-fi furry. Whether it's how to write your epic space opera or your quick jaunt into the very possible future, this is the place to bring your cybernetics into our parallel world.

items.title: Writing Foundations 101
items.description: A starting talk to attend if you’re just beginning to put some words to paper or maybe you’ve written a few stories and you want to expand your craft. We’ll discuss some of the basics of plot structure, ways to improve writing, and how to benefit from the heirarchy of talent in writing. Seasoned writers are also welcome for review or adding experienced opinions.

items.title: Kid Internet (Thursday Nightly Dance)
items.description:
Every night of the convention, our main stage transforms into a world of lights, effects, and electronic music.

Thursday (Friday Morning) 1:30am to 2:30am
DJ: Kid Internet
Genre: hardcore mix
items.location: DLCC - Spirit of Pittsburgh Ballroom (Main Events)

items.title: TRANSISTORS (Sunday Dead Dog Dance)
items.description:
Every night of the convention, our main stage transforms into a world of lights, effects, and electronic music.

Sunday 10pm to 11pm
DJ: TRANSISTORS (Morganite x DRAC-0)
Genre: drum & bass
items.location: DLCC - Spirit of Pittsburgh Ballroom (Main Events)

items.title: Sovereign Eclipse
items.description: Come test out a new Furry sci-fi game being written by SteelTheWarrior (Mass Effect: Unity, Freedom Fighters & Your Imagined Furry Fantasy). Choose from a massive assortment of species and genetic culture combinations, and explore an immensely dynamic and complex new universe full of mystery, intrigue and betrayal!

items.title: How to Start a Furry Con
items.description: Have you ever wanted to start a furry convention but didn’t know where to begin? Come by this panel and our experienced panelists will discuss the ins, outs, and what-have-yous of starting a con.

items.title: Beastcub Suiters Meet
items.description: Do you own or know anyone who owns a Beastcub suit? Come together for a fursuit family meetup and photo!

items.title: Rad Asian Furry Meet and Greet
items.description: Are you Asian? Or you think you are one? Or you want to get to make more Asian friends? Then come join us for our Asian furry meet and greet! Come connect with fellow Asian furries, explore Asian furry cultures, discover the all the weird Asian secrets, and most importantly have an absolute blast and fun time at Anthrocon!

items.title: Mature Art Auction (Setup)
items.description: Setup for the Mature Art Show Auction

items.title: SCP & PPAC Pet Romp
items.description:
Steel City Pups (SCP) and Pittsburgh Paws & Claws (PPAC) present: A Pet Romp!
What's a romp, you ask? An event specifically geared to pet players and handlers of all types to come out and engage in their headspaces with others. We'll have a plethora of toys to play with, and mats to play on, so make sure to bring your kneepads and gear, a valid ID you can show us at the door to verify you're 18+, and your beautiful smiles! We'll have staff on hand to help keep things fun and answer any questions you may have, to come on out and help put the "play" in pet play.

items.title: Martial Arts Meet and Greet
items.description:
What is it about martial arts that makes us stick with and suffer through endless training? Are we in it because it's a nerd-friendly sport? Fantasy and historic cosplay? Self-defense? Magic and mystery? Fitness with style? Something else?

Come to listen, talk, show off your favorite moves. I'll bring my gear.

Your host is a former USANKF competitor and tournament official, but all styles are welcome.

items.title: Escape Room ($)
items.description: Furry Logic is proud to bring back Cyberpunk Escape, previously showcased at conventions in 2020-2021! Players work together to untangle the corporate conspiracies and expose Megacorp for its wrongdoings from the inside - but act swiftly since the jamming drones can only protect you for so long before you are exposed to be traced and erased! Games are 60 minutes long, and spots will be limited! Tickets are $30 per person, with a portion of all tickets supporting charity! Rooms A and B are identical. Want to just play with family or friends? Book a private room by paying for a minimum of 5 tickets in an empty slot. Got a big group? Split into two teams and play head-to-head in our identical rooms! Please check out our online booking website https://www.bookeo.com/furrylogicllc to get your tickets early, or, check out our sales table on site for more info! If you have any questions, reach out to us at furrylogicllc@gmail.com

items.title: NOVOCANINE (Saturday Nightly Dance)
items.description:
Every night of the convention, our main stage transforms into a world of lights, effects, and electronic music.

Saturday (Sunday Morning) 1:30am to 2:30am
DJ: NOVOCANINE
Genre: drum & bass
items.location: DLCC - Spirit of Pittsburgh Ballroom (Main Events)

items.title: Tumblr Meetup (18+)
items.description:
“Quit telling everyone I’m dead!”
Are you still stuck on this hellsite? Stole shoelaces from the President? Is Goncharov on your top films list? Do you relate pineapples and werewolves? I know there’s still plenty of furries on tumblr and I feel like we should meet up! Unfortunately we can’t guarantee a ball pit.

items.title: Charity Comedy Show
items.description:
As the con rolls onward, come support the charity with a good old fashioned comedy show!


This is a ticketed event with proceeds benefitting this year's Charity.
items.location: DLCC - Spirit of Pittsburgh Ballroom (Main Events)

items.title: Your Imagined Furry Fantasy 18+
items.description: This light-hearted RPG system was borne of frisky fantasy, silly stories of over-the-top shenanigans, inspired by popular flash games like Corruption of Champions, Trials in Tainted Space and Flexible Survival! Gameplay gives players a different perspective on the psychology behind modern-day sexuality and taboo as they adventure in a world where just about everything is over-sexualized (including combat!) to offer silly-fun and sex-positive escapades!

items.title: Puppet Workshop: Parade Puppet
items.description: Join us for an advanced puppet fabrication workshop making a giant parade puppet! This large group puppet will be performed in the Fursuit Parade on Saturday! Participants will be expected to know how to use scissors and hot glue guns. Join us to learn how to pattern and construct a puppet at a LARGE scale. This is a great opportunity to learn new skills, experiment with a large artform, and meet fellow puppetry enthusiasts. If you’re interested in puppeteering for the parade please come help us build! Once we’ve finished building the puppet we’ll take it for a spin at the dance!

items.title: Adult Writing, Kink
items.description:
Writing adult fiction is not always as simple as the standard expectations. In our community, there are plenty of strange desires that can be encountered. Discovering the secrets of such kinks in writing can be rewarding artistically and financially and downright fun, but there are always challenges. Join us for an open discussion of the odder side of adult writing.
Must be over 18, or over the legal age in country of citizenship. IDs and badges will be checked at the door.

items.title: VA Benefits For You and Your Families
items.description: In this interactive panel we will go over the different benefits that the VA offers to veterans, spouses, and their families and how to properly apply for them.

items.title: Fanwing Meetup! (Wings of Fire)
items.description: Are you a fan of Tui T. Sutherland's hit book series? Do you know your IceWings from your RainWings? Come hang out with your fellow dragons! With Arc 4 in full swing and a show on the way, we have much to discuss.

items.title: BigBlueFox (Saturday Nightly Dance)
items.description:
Every night of the convention, our main stage transforms into a world of lights, effects, and electronic music.

Saturday 9:30pm to 10:30pm
DJ: BigBlueFox
Genre: tech house
items.location: DLCC - Spirit of Pittsburgh Ballroom (Main Events)

items.title: Blackjack and Hoofers: Hoofer Bleat and Greet
items.description:
Are you a Hoofer or Hoofer-adjacent?

Goats, deers, horses, sheep… if you have horns or hooves, come on by!

items.title: Vinyl Furs Meet & Greet
items.description: This is opportunity for those who enjoy vinyl records, whether it's collecting, listening, or just curious, to meet and talk about this wonderful hobby.

items.title: DLCC - Rooftop Hours
items.description: The DLCC Rooftop closes to attendees at 11pm sharp. Attendees must leave the rooftop and overlooks by that time.

items.title: K-Pop Random Play Dance
items.description: Are you a K-Pop stan? Maybe you've learned some choreography of your favorite groups? Come join us as we play random K-Pop songs, and you dance! Freestyling is also encouraged.

items.title: Furry Text Adventures
items.description: Gather round, literally, and make your way through some old school text adventures! Draggor, your host, will be subbing in for the computer, so sassy replies are to be expected from sassy commands.  Imagine a classic text adventure but as a whole audience cooperative game!

items.title: ADA Hangout
items.description:
Come relax, chat and share stories, and make friends in our ADA safe space. We'll talk about the positive things the con has done to be ADA friendly, while discussing what could be improved and how for next year.

Feel free to bring your emotional support plushies! Plushies are a vital tool for many of us ADA folks to express ourselves and find comfort when overwhelmed.

items.title: Digimon TCG
items.description: Curious about the Digimon Trading Card Game, or just love picking up new games fast? Come learn the basics of the 2020 Digimon TCG and see why it’s become a favorite among competitive and casual players alike. We’ll cover core rules, strategies, and what makes the game so enjoyable to pick up and play. Test decks will be available for new players to try real matches, and experienced tamers are encouraged to pair up, play, and help others learn. No matter your familiarity with the Digital World, you’re welcome to sit down, shuffle up, and enjoy the game together.

items.title: K-Nyne’s Dance Battling 101
items.description: ver been curious to know more about what dance battles are all about? Have you ever wondered about what to do when you’re on the big stage? Let this be the gateway for knowledge! This workshop is taught by K-Nyne , one of the members of the AC 2023 Floor Wars' winning team: FF 15

items.title: Winter Powersports
items.description: Winter powersports meet up

items.title: Fuzzy Tales Presents: Camera, Action, Darkness
items.description: Welcome to Appalachia in the 1990s as you and your friends come to chase down a local legend to put on film for the World to see that exists in the wilderness nearby. Despite the rumors of previous people vanishing in their pursuit, that was not going to stop you from an Oscar winning film. Will you find the Creature and create your winner or vanish like the others before you.

items.title: Super Smash Bros. Ultimate - Qualifiers ($)
items.description:
Paws of Fury returns for 2026 with 7 charity gaming events! Event #7 is the biggest of them all, Super Smash Bros. Ultimate!

This tournament takes place across two phases. All players must register and participate in the qualifier phase in order to reach brackets, which is scheduled for 8 PM on Saturday.

Entry Fee: $10 charitable donation
Prizes for the top 4 places
All players are guaranteed one set of three matches, now with scheduled play to make sure your convention time is respected!

Full Event Details and Registration: https://www.start.gg/tournament/paws-of-fury-2026-at-anthrocon/event/super-smash-bros-ultimate-qualifiers
(Note: You must have your con badge number to register for the event)

The bracket phase will be streamed at http://www.twitch.tv/AnthroconGaming

items.title: The Art of Viola Performance
items.description:
Have you ever wondered how those weird sticks made sound when they rub against metal strings? Are you a beginner player looking to see just how wild these techniques get? Or are you a violinist planning on coming here to make fun of an "inferior" instrument?

I'll be talking about (and demonstrating) how to produce sound, the nuances between a violin and viola, the breadth of techniques possible on the viola, some short solos + analysis, and more! Hope to see ya here :3

items.title: Zine Trading
items.description: Do YOU make zines? Do you collect zines? Do you even know what a zine is? Drop by and trade zines with other zinesters and artists, maybe pick up your very first zine, or get some tips on how to make a zine!

items.title: Remembering “Anthrocoon” (Bob Nelson)
items.description:
We will honor and pay tribute to a “FURst Fandom” Furry who loved Anthrocon and attended each year from its humble beginnings in Albany, NY 1997 to his last Anthrocon in 2024 - a few months before he suffered a heart attack which led to his untimely death on January 4th, 2025 at age 62.

He loved Anthrocon and Pittsburgh so much, Bob’s fursona name reflected his dual love for Raccoons and this con. Bob was featured in Joe Strike’s book “FURRY NATION,” hosted panels at Anthrocon and various Northeast U.S. furry conventions.

Bob “Anthrocoon” hosted the “Classic Cartoons” panel at AC for many years, and co-hosted other panels focused on Greymuzzles and Disney’s Zootopia. 
We will honor “Anthrocoon” with photos, videos and guest interaction. Anyone who knew Bob is welcome to attend and say a few words. 

If it wasn’t for “Anthrocoon” - I wouldn’t had attended Anthrocon 2024 “AnthroCoaster”. 

This is my tribute to an amazing Friend and Furry.

items.title: Staying Sober in the Furry Fandom
items.description: An open table conversation about staying sober in the furry fandom. Talk about your story, your tips and tricks, and gain connections to those in the community!

items.title: Saturday Cocktail Cruise
items.description:
Ticketed passengers only - Your Receipt from your purchase in ConCat IS Your ticket. You will not receive a separate booking ticket. Simply show it to our Anthrocon staff assisting with Boarding.

There will be no delay of departure!

items.title: MiSTa3K (MST3K) with Alkali
items.description: Mystery Science Theatre 3000 – furry style! Come join Alkali as we (and probably the whole room full of people) verbally rip a movie of our choosing to shreds! Hopefully this year’s movie will NOT have center screen wipes.

items.title: Fiddledo Presents: The Video Game Music Challenge
items.description: Will you be crowned the victor of the Video Game Music Challenge? Running since 2017, the Video Game Music Challenge is a video game music trivia game show where everyone from the panelists to the audience can participate! Can you guess what game the song is from? Featuring unique categories, laughs, and prizes! Test your video game music knowhow!

items.title: P.A.W.S. On CPR
items.description: Have you ever been worried about what to do in a medical emergency? With P.A.W.S. on CPR, you can learn compression only CPR, how to use an AED, and more from EMT and nationally certified CPR instructor CopperSprite. Come get "paws" on experience with mannequins as we go through how to perform life saving actions to help provide a safer future.

items.title: Fursuit Maker Meet and Greet
items.description: Do you make fursuits? Whether you make suits for yourself or make fursuits professionally, come meet up with other fursuit makers!

items.title: Charity D&D with Alkali
items.description: A D&D game like you have never seen before. The story is set, the goal is achievable, and all of you can help. Heavy audience participation panel. And remember, if you have to roll the dice, you have already lost.

items.title: Zoids TCG & .hack TCG block
items.description:
Zoids - "A hybrid tactical tabletop card game set within the Zoids universe.
Learn to play, pick your arsenal, dominate the battlefield!

Join our Telegram: ZoidsFans"

.hack//enemy - "Learn to play this forgotten TCG with fellow Dot Hackers!
Log-in, gear up, fight monsters!
Join our Telegram: .hack//Furs"

items.title: Opening Ceremonies
items.location: DLCC - Spirit of Pittsburgh Ballroom (Main Events)

items.title: Free Miniature/DND Paint & Take!
items.description:
Provided by Chronicle RPGs & Zenith's Outer Reach; Come paint with us! Whether you're a complete beginner or an established maestro, join us for a free miniature and all supplies provided. Take part for as long as you like with drop-in tables commencing from 5pm! 

Scan the QR code to preview the Zenith's Outer Reach Kickstarter, or copy the link here: https://www.backerkit.com/call_to_action/f895d526-7872-41e5-94c1-a010a6ec5898/landing?preview_mode=true

items.title: Are You Afured of The Dark?
items.description: Join us for a selection of Cryptid Stories and Scary Tales!

items.title: Puppet Workshop: SNOOFS
items.description: THIS PANEL REQUIRES SEWING EXPERIENCE. Join us to sew up some fuzzy hand and rod puppets with tons of personality (and optional arms)! The Snoof is a fun and free puppet pattern pioneered by Adam Kreutinger. This is an advanced beginners workshop for eager puppeteers looking to make a fuzzy hand puppet of their very own! This panel will have supplies for 20 participants and will require some sewing experience and the ability to work with hot glue guns. All children need to work with an adult guardian, as this puppet requires the use of needles, scissors, and hot glue. Get ready for a lip sync sing along at the end of the workshop to break in those new little critters!

items.title: Westin - Gaming Room Hours
items.description: These hours are hours of operation for all gaming spaces in the Westin hotel, 2nd floor.

items.title: Plushie Hospital
items.description: Plushie Hospital is a hands-on, interactive lesson where you can learn all about the care and repair of stuffed animals. The first hour of the panel is a lesson and Q+A, and the second hour is a hands-on workshop where you can practice the techniques that you learned about. All supplies are provided - you just need to bring a plushie friend to work on!

items.title: Werewolves, Shapeshifters & Animal Transformation
items.description: From the Epic of Gilgamesh to Turning Red, tales of humans turning into animals have become part of our shared cultural heritage. Join us as we look at shapeshifters from different cultures like the more familiar werewolves, kitsune, tanuki, and selkie to the lesser-known ones like the bouda, adze, tikbalang, and kushtaka. How Dracula and Hollywood reshaped our perspective of werewolves. And the common tropes and narrative structures across these tales from old folklore to modern movies. Christian werewolves, Animorphs, transformation webcomics, the TF community, and more! Also a sneak preview of our new comic series Shifter School!

items.title: Devil's Advocate: How to Write a "Good" Villain
items.description: A hero is only as good as their villain, and the best villains are the ones we love to hate. But how do you avoid the pitfalls of making your villain too flat, or too lame, or “evil for the sake of being evil”? In this lecture we’ll discuss the morals, motives, and modus opperandi of some famous villains, the nuances of antiheros, antagonists, and villains, and how to cut Satan a break—there are much more interesting and sinister figures for inspiration for your big baddies. A Q and A will follow.

items.title: DIY Leathercraft: Leather Collars
items.description: In this panel, we will teach you the basics of leatherworking and guide you through making a personalized leather collar. There will be a 25 dollar materials fee to participate, but any may observe for free.

items.title: Thursday Sightseeing Cruise
items.description:
Ticketed passengers only - Your receipt from your purchase in ConCat IS Your ticket. You will not receive a separate booking ticket. Simply show it to our Anthrocon staff assisting with boarding.

There will be no delay of departure!

items.title: Ham Furs - Amateur Radio Exam
items.description: Are you interested in gaining your FCC license to operate on amateur radio "ham" bands, and join the thriving community of furry amateur radio operators? Then come out to our annual FCC licensing exam. Our team of furry radio enthusiasts and authorized examiners will be conducting a full course of Technician, General, and Amateur Extra exams (staffing dependent), and would love to see you there. Please note, the FCC and Volunteer Exam Coordinator service regulations require us to collect a $15 exam fee from each test taker. This collected fee goes to the VEC and the exam team does not retain these funds. Venmo or Cash are accepted.

items.title: Backstage Tour
items.description: Join us for an in-depth backstage tour of everything that makes A/V work at Anthrocon. Learn about our sound system, audio consoles, lighting fixtures, consoles, truss and rigging, video cameras, broadcast switching gear, streaming infrastructure, and so much more. Stay for a Q&A session with skilled A/V staff that volunteer their time to make Anthrocon's panels, concerts, dances, and competitions look more amazing each year.
items.location: DLCC - Spirit of Pittsburgh Ballroom (Main Events)

items.title: The Making of Yiff! Magazine
items.description:
Life sure can feel like a pie in the face these days, huh?

Yiff! Magazine is a funny animal periodical modeled after early furry amateur press associations. Yiff! aims to platform great work that might be lost in the shuffle of social media, be that unmitigated kink or nuanced discussions of hot button fandom issues (I read it for the articles, I swear!). This panel will give you an idea of what we do, and some ways you can do it too!

This panel follows the release of our third issue, "Tooned In!" It's all about the ways toons imitate life and vice versa. In addition to going over the conception of this issue, we will be going in-depth on some of the work within. Come get your hands on some Yiff!

items.title: AWOO Presents: A Collection of Furry Readings
items.description: The Anthropomorphic Writers Outreach Organization welcomes you to Anthrocon 2026! Join us for a session of readings by various acclaimed authors in the fandom and maybe find a new favorite!

items.title: Stellaris: Furry Automated Luxury Gay Space Comm.
items.description: Map gamers unite! Come learn the basics of the most accessible Paradox map-painter and sci-fi multiplayer 4X roleplaying game. We break down the game's systems and how to dip your toes in them to create the space-faring furry empire of your dreams (or nightmares). No experience required; only interest in SPAAACE!

items.title: Historical Furs Show and Tell
items.description: The influence of history on art and literature is enormous, even when furries are involved.  Join us for a show and tell where we’ll discuss our historically-inspired fursuits, cosplays, and fursonas.  Art and artifacts are also welcome, provided that they follow all convention policies, safety rules, and standards of conduct.

items.title: Scouting in the Furry Fandom
items.description: Have you ever been involved in scouting? Have you ever wanted to meet other furs who were involved in scouting as well? Here's your chance! Stop by and meet fellow scouts from around the fandom. Share stories, experiences, and meet new friends! Scouts of all nations and ranks welcome!

items.title: Intro to Ballet
items.description:
Are you fascinated by ballet? Would you like to learn how to turn, jump, balance, or move with grace?

Hello! My name's Wolfgang and I'm a husky who really likes ballet! I've trained with conservatories all over the country and I want to bring some of this knowledge to the fandom. 

If you are interested in ballet, this is a crash course and sampler of sorts. In this class I will talk about the history and culture of ballet, as well as give a class suited for all levels (with some more advanced practices in the end). Please arrive early and warm up, as there is a lot to cover, and I don't want anyone to get injured!

items.title: What is Animalism?
items.description: This panel discusses animalism and other philosophies that explore human beings' connection to animals. Come learn how different cultures and ideologies view humans' relation to animals. This is a lecture style panel that will teach you some cool words to appear 'smart' at parties.

items.title: Writing Habits and Staying Productive
items.description: Writing is challenging at the best of times, but throw in social life, a day job, and a family and it can be extremely difficult. How do you keep going with your attention being pulled in so many different directions? Our panelists offer up some perspective and ways to keep yourself going when the words aren't getting on the page like they should.

items.title: Golden Treasure: The Lost Draak
items.description: A small TTRPG by SteelTheWarrior made in tribute to the inspiring and breathtaking game “Golden Treasure: The Great Green”.    Players will take the form of a group of Draak-Kin hatched amid the chaos of their nestlands being torn asunder by No-Tailed creatures that pay no respect to the Nature they take from. The team will find No-Tails, predators, starvation, mazes, treacherous weather and even competing Draak all standing between the hatchlings and hope for survival!  Will they survive, or feed the cycle?

items.title: Tiktok/Short Form Content Furs Meetup
items.description: From the FYP to AC, come Meetup with fellow TikTok and short form content creators and enjoyers! Make fun videos, collaborate with fellow furs, share ideas and tips on video making and more!

items.title: Fursuiting at Sporting Events
items.description: Are you a sports fanatic? Are you looking for a fun way to support your favorite team? Public fursuiting has been happening for quite some time, but in the last few years, furries have begun to take over sporting events! Come learn all about the dos and don'ts of public suiting at your favorite team's home games!

items.title: Designing Fantasy Furry Arms and Armor
items.description: This panel will teach you about arms and armor in reality and fantasy, and leave you with a foundation for understanding how to better design armor and equipment for your own personal furry fantasy setting.

items.title: Dragon Meet
items.description: A place for dragons or dragon appreciators to come and mingle and meet one another.

items.title: The Hope Panel - PCD & Your Next Big Adventure
items.description: Feeling down about the end of the con? Come take stock of an amazing weekend and use proven techniques to beat post-con depression and start your next great adventure!

items.title: Touch This! Fur, Fabrics, and Foams: Understanding
items.description: Ready to level up your builds? This hands-on, interactive panel is perfect for makers of all experience levels, whether you're crafting your first cosplay, building a fursuit, or designing a puppet for performance. Learn what materials you need for your projects, and come swatch your fursona with us! Success starts with knowing your materials.

items.title: Dealers Room & Con Store Hours
items.description: The Dealers Room will open to Ultrasponsors and Supersponsors at 9:50am. All other badge levels will be able to enter at 10:00am

items.title: Breaking 101 (AKA Breakdancing)
items.description: Curious about Breaking? This 101 class will introduce some basics welcoming to all skill levels and ages! This workshop is taught by Ddog, one of the members of the AC 2025 Floor Wars' winning team: Kingdom Arts Crew.

items.title: Midnight City (Friday Nightly Dance)
items.description:
Every night of the convention, our main stage transforms into a world of lights, effects, and electronic music.

Friday (Saturday Morning) 1:30am to 2:30am
DJ: Midnight City
Genre: neurofunk, dancefloor drum & bass
items.location: DLCC - Spirit of Pittsburgh Ballroom (Main Events)

items.title: Let's $ew: Nub Tails
items.description: Come join the nub club! This paid beginner friendly sewing panel will provide all materials and tools needed to create a fursuit grade nub tail. Go to https://www.snep.works/panels/nubac to sign up!  $45, tax included, includes a take home sewing kit

items.title: Clicker Training and its Misuse in the Fandom
Advice from an Amateur Artist: How I Taught Myself
items.description: An intro to drawing fundamentals to form a path towards your art journey. Learn about free beginner practices, teachers, and resources available for self taught artists. From lines to anatomy, come see what you can apply to your doodles to brighten someone’s day!

items.title: Curses to Cures:  History of Public Health
items.description: Disease has always felt like something other — a curse, a creature, a cryptic punishment from forces beyond our control. This panel explores how that ancient fear slowly transformed into something powerful: the idea that sickness is not fate, but a problem communities can solve together. It's a history full of monsters, mistakes, and hard-won miracles.

items.title: Orchestra Rehearsal Blockout
items.description: This hidden event blocks out an extra half hour before and after the published Orchestra rehearsal time so there's time to setup and clear the room.

items.title: Make a Tail with Glitzy Fox and Pinku
items.description: Have you wanted to get into fursuit making but don't know where to start? Join us for a one of a kind experience where you can create and sew your very own small fursuit tail. All materials will be provided. Sponsored by Big Z Fabric. Please note there will be a cap on attendance.

items.title: Beware! Calcium Beyond These Trees-Skull Furs Meet
items.description:
One day at your local tavern (or your catering of choice if you're not much of a tavern person), you heard the rumor from a quiet table that one spot in the vast woods that cover the DLCC was planned to be the hotspot of peculiar animals around this time. Out of curiosity, you decided to investigate these claims and made your way to the supposed meeting spot and discovered that these creatures are none other than skull furs, creatures that--on the inside--aren't unlike you and I!

You'll experience the following at this little meet & greet:
-An opportunity to make new friends, reconnect with old friends, and make memories all around. Be sociable!
-You can collect little keepsakes that will be distributed by the hosts, whether it be ribbons, stickers, or more!
-Not everyone there will be a skull fur, nor is that a requirement. Everyone is welcome!
-Group photo guaranteed during the meet!
-Sign the community banner brought by one generous host and make your mark on a piece of skull furs history!

You now had one more question to ask and answer yourself: will you go over there and greet them as well?

items.title: Werewolf
items.description: Werewolf is a social deduction game where the town tries to run out the werewolves living among them. Some townsfolk will have special powers while others just try to live their lives. Through cleaver reasoning and trust in the correct person you can weed out the werewolves or the werewolf pack could sleep with full bellies.

items.title: Inanimate Transformation
items.description:
A Look at Statues, Pool Toys, Plushies, and More

While human to animal transformation is common in stories, human to objects are much more rare. Often they fall down the path of nightmare horror fuel or comedic gag. However, in the furry fandom there has been a new wave of inanimate TF in the form of pool toys, plushie, and living fursuits. Join us for a look at the history of inanimate transformation including: Medusa, Lot’s Wife, I Have No Mouth and I Must Scream, Twilight Zone, Turbo Teen, Beauty and the Beast, Gleipnir, Pickle Rick, Reborn as a Vending Machine, furry pool toys, and of course all those wacky kids' commercials from the 1990s to 2000s. What are the psychology, themes, and messages of such works and where can such stories take us?

items.title: Authenticity, Society, and Furries
items.description: There's a lot of advice out there about personal growth, and individualistic pursuits of a healthier way of living as your authentic self. While this approach can be helpful, the speaker believes it's missing an important piece of context about this pursuit: the society we live in and the ideas it has placed in our heads about how we "should" live, and how these ideas can be at odds with a more authentic, attuned way of living. This panel aims to present the rest of the context, because with understanding comes empowerment. Knowing what we've learned empowers us to choose what to unlearn, and also to form communities that reflect our authentic values in a world that seems determined to suppress them. Questions and audience engagement encouraged!

I will discuss the coalitions I built, my work in the depths of City Hall, and how something as simple as a traffic cone can be the result of months or years of debate. I had no formal training and made it up as I went along, but my city is now funding $50,000 for a safe bike lane study. I will teach you how to advocate in your city or town.

items.title: Choosing and Playing a New Game System
items.description:
Choosing and playing a new fantasy role-playing game (RPG) or video game title is an exciting journey into uncharted worlds filled with magic, mystery, and adventure. The process often begins with exploration—researching different titles, reading reviews, watching gameplay previews, and considering recommendations from friends or gaming communities. Players may be drawn to rich storytelling like that found in The Witcher 3: Wild Hunt, expansive open worlds such as The Elder Scrolls V: Skyrim, or challenging, lore-driven experiences like Elden Ring. For tabletop fantasy RPGs like Dungeons & Dragons, this includes collaborating with other players, creating backstories, and engaging in imaginative storytelling.

From learning the game’s mechanics, developing a character build, and understanding the rules of the world can be both challenging and rewarding to personal preferences that play a major role,  this panel will discuss some of the priorities players seek that ultimately leads to selecting and playing a new fantasy RPG is about discovering a world that sparks imagination, offers meaningful challenges, and provides memorable experiences.

items.title: Worldbuilding: How to Make a Story Bible
items.description: Whether your story is set in galaxy far, far away, a fantasy continent worlds apart, or in a world we know and love in a book or in a tabletop game, a Story Bible is a tool that helps any author organize the aspects of their world-building—the cultures, rules of magic and/or science, weather, politics, ecology, economy, timelines, character interviews and designs, and more. In this lecture we will talk about acquiring the right kind of book, what kind of materials to use, what kind of information to include and how to organize it depending on the kind of story you are writing, and how to use your bible to add layers of depth to your characters and stories. Q and A will follow.

items.title: An Introvert's and Autistic's Con Survival Guide
items.description: Furry conventions are incredible for community and creativity, but the bright lights, loud fursuit dances, and constant social interaction can lead to "con-burnout" fast. Whether you’re navigating your first con or your fiftieth, join us for a deep dive into practical strategies for sensory management, social pacing, and self-advocacy. We’ll cover everything from finding "quiet zones" and the "rule of 6-2-1," to managing "post-con blues" and how to politely bow out of a conversation when your social battery hits 1%.

items.title: Don’t Panic! How to Save Your Costume (and Sanity)
items.description:
My business partner and I are experienced costume makers with a strong foundation in costume design and wearable technology. Both of us hold a Bachelor of Arts Degree in Costume Design and Technology, with a focus on construction methods, materials, and ensuring costumes are both safe and comfortable. I, Lupus Wolf, am currently getting a MFA in Costume Technology. Together, we bring years of hands-on experience, one has been building costumes since 2018, and the other since 2021, with particular attention to material testing, mobility, and durability.

As the founders of Wear-Wolves, we specialize in custom fursuits, puppets, and foam-based builds. Through our projects, we’ve gained valuable insight into how materials and costumes perform in busy convention settings, and how to combine different materials from various creative communities into unique solutions for costume builds.

We love to give back to the maker community by sharing lessons learned and troubleshooting techniques through panels, workshops, and online conversations, aiming to make things easier for new makers by sharing what we wish we’d known at the beginning.

items.description: Wanting to know more about how to be an inflation artist? Interested in learning how to draw silly furry characters BIG and ROUND??? Well this panel is sure to tickle your fancy!! Join Kuro, otherwise known by the artist name Gatsby in the furry inflation community, as he talks about some tips and tricks on how to master creating the many shapes found in the world of blimped up characters. items.title: Collaboration Confuzilication
items.description:
Can you draw, doodle or scribble funny animals? Are you quick-witted? Play well with others? Then ""Collaboration Confuzilication!"" is for you! 

Here's how it works: every player receives a formatted, 4-panel sheet of paper; a word will be chosen at random, and each player will have twelve minutes to draw a picture in the first panel based on that word. Then everyone passes their paper onto the person seated next to them, and a new word is selected for those people to draw a picture based on the new word for the second panel. 

Sounds easy? Well, it’s not – because the new picture ALSO has to continue the story started in the previous panel! The process is repeated for the third and fourth panels until the page is filled with a masterpiece of collective creativity (and weirdness!). 

We’ll supply that sheet of paper, but please bring your own pens, pencils, and erasers. Participation is limited to the first 40 people to arrive, but everyone is welcome to stay, observe the action (or take over from an artist who has to leave early) and enjoy the finished collaborations!

items.description: Are you a fan of fish? Maybe fish themed fursuits, cosplays, or items? This is the right meet for you! Whether you're a BarkShark, Orca, Eel, or just a fan, come and meet other fish enthusiasts in this fish-tacular themed Fursuit Meet!

items.title: Anthrocon Poker Championship - Day 1 ($)
items.description:
Paws of Fury returns for 2026 with 7 charity gaming events! Event #4 is the 11th annual Anthrocon Poker Championship! Can you dodge bullets to win this year's title and add your name to the Silver Water Dish?

Day 1 of the event will play down to our "July Nine," with the final table taking place Sunday at Noon.

Entry Fee: $20 charitable donation
30 dog tags will be available for purchase for $30, and provide one rebuy
Prizes for the top 9 places

Full Event Details and Registration: https://www.start.gg/tournament/paws-of-fury-2026-at-anthrocon/event/anthrocon-poker-championship
(Note: You must have your con badge number to register for the event, and you must be 18 years of age or older.)

items.title: Short Form Content Creation 101
items.description: Come join TikTok creator Mason Crow for an exciting and informative presentation on the basics (and not-so-basics) of short-form content creation! This discussion will focus on the various ways that you can build a dedicated community, monetize your platform, and make videos that genuinely stand out. Any/all levels of experience are welcome!

items.title: Junk Journaling - Furry Edition!
items.description:
Furry conventions leave attendees with a lot of "junk"—whether it is used business cards, informative flyers, free stickers, receipts, or something else—many things end up in the trash. 

What if there was a way to collage this "junk" to commemorate your convention experience? 

Whether you've seen junk journaling in practice on social media or never heard of it before— this workshop is for you! We will turn your junk into beautiful pieces of art. During this workshop we will show examples of items to use both inside and outside of conventions and go over composition and techniques to make your junk journal your own. 

While the first portion of this panel will be informational, the second portion will allow participants to make their own junk journal page(s). If you wish to participate in the workshop portion, please bring your own supplies (a journal/paper, glue sticks, scissors, and random junk!), as very limited supplies will be available for participant use.

items.description: Furry Logic is proud to bring back Cyberpunk Escape, previously showcased at conventions in 2020-2021! Players work together to untangle the corporate conspiracies and expose Megacorp for its wrongdoings from the inside - but act swiftly since the jamming drones can only protect you for so long before you are exposed to be traced and erased! Want to just play with family or friends? Book a private room by paying for a minimum of 5 tickets in an empty slot. Got a big group?
